$QCOM plans to bring parts of its new data center chip architecture to smartphones, PCs and cars

Its High Bandwidth Compute architecture stacks memory and compute vertically to improve data flow potentially enabling more local AI models and always-on agents across edge devices.

Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo says $AAPL is reportedly raising baseline iPhone DRAM from 8GB to 9GB in 2027 to keep Apple Intelligence running smoothly.

On-device AI is pushing DRAM content per phone higher which is bullish for $MU, Samsung and SK Hynix.
$PLTR UP OVER 5% TODAY

$RKLB was selected by NASA for three Electron missions including two PolSIR launches and one TSIS-2 launch from New Zealand in early 2027.

The awards add more government-backed visibility to Electron and reinforce Rocket Lab’s role as a trusted NASA launch provider.

You can watch my full $MSFT breakdown here:

piped.video/PdiXuUNDNcI?si=fHfU…

The market is underappreciating that $MSFT already owns the enterprise workflow layer where AI adoption is likely to happen.

Workers already live inside Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, GitHub and Azure which gives Microsoft a built-in distribution advantage as AI gets embedded across the enterprise.

Copilot is still early but the numbers are getting more meaningful with over 20M paid seats, paid seat additions up 250% YoY and customers with more than 50K seats quadrupling YoY

Every 10M Copilot seats at ~$30 per user per month represents ~$3.6B of annualized revenue so if Copilot scales to 100M seats over the next several years then it becomes a major software revenue layer on top of Microsoft 365.
Michael Burry just bought $MSFT leaps expiring in December 2028 with a $700 strike.

$ON is down over 20% after announcing a $7B all-stock deal to acquire $SYNA and expand its edge AI and physical AI portfolio.

The deal adds AI compute and connectivity, targets $200M in annual synergies and is expected to become earnings accretive within 18 months.

FUTURUM EQUITIES PODCAST EPISODE #47

Daniel and I will be live tomorrow at 1PM ET exclusively on the Futurum Equities YouTube channel covering: 

• $CBRS & $MU earnings and what they say about next stage of AI infrastructure
• Why memory may have permanently re-rated into scarce AI infrastructure with no relief until 2028
• How memory bottleneck is becoming a tax on every AI workload and what that means for $MSFT, $AMZN, $META, $ORCL  & $GOOGL
• Why I've been buying $PLTR at new 52-week lows despite rising OpenAI & Anthropic fears

We’ll leave the last 10 minutes for live Q&A so bring your questions.

$SPCX is reportedly considering a direct-to-consumer Starlink mobile service in the U.S., which could put it in direct competition with $VZ, $T and $TMUS.

SpaceX is trying to move beyond carrier partnerships and turn Starlink into a full consumer connectivity platform.

I think $PLTR has been getting hit because investors are worried about competition from OpenAI and Anthropic but I’ve been been adding to my position because I think a lot of people have this backwards.

LLMs are obviously extremely valuable but as the major models continue to improve, they're also starting to cluster together in performance and because they're generally non-deterministic it means the real enterprise challenge becomes more about making sure that answer is grounded in the right data, tied to the right workflow, approved by the right human and executed in the right operational system.

I truly think the concern that OpenAI and Anthropic could move into Palantir’s market is more modest than people assume. Model companies are focused on the model and building intelligence while Palantir is trying to help enterprises turn that intelligence into operational outcomes (very different lanes).

$AMZN is reportedly raising GPU instance prices by 20% next month which is another sign that AI compute scarcity is turning into pricing power across the infrastructure stack.

$NVDA captures the silicon margin, $MU captures HBM and DRAM pricing, AWS captures the instance markup and AI application companies are left absorbing the cost.
Cathie Wood bought ~$19M of $CBRS yesterday.

Hormuz traffic sees a sharp d/d uptick

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings rose to 70 on 24 June, up 105% day on day, as demining efforts advanced and operators increasingly used the Omani route. Commercial traffic accounted for most activity, with 53 transits, while low-risk vessels dominated the day’s profile. The US-Iran MoU framework and apparent lifting of the US blockade appear to have supported a short-term confidence boost, although IRGC warnings against use of the Omani route could create a new source of contention.

IMO-route use picked up slightly, but continued Dark routing, incomplete demining and unresolved issues over inspections, sanctions and future Strait governance mean the rebound is not yet a confirmed return to pre-crisis conditions.
